<!-- vault:start:conventions -->
## Conventions (from code-vault)

The following are inlined from `../code-vault/conventions/`. The vault is the source of truth — re-run `bootstrap.sh` to refresh.

### Branch naming


# Branch naming

## Iron rule

**Never commit directly to `main`.** Always create a branch, push, and open a PR. No exceptions, even for trivial changes.

## Naming format

```
<type>/<kebab-case-slug>
```

Where `<type>` matches the allowed [Conventional Commits](git-conventional-commits.md) types most commonly:

- `feat/`  — new feature
- `fix/`   — bug fix
- `chore/` — maintenance, deps, config
- `docs/`  — documentation
- `refactor/` — restructuring without behavior change

## Examples

- `feat/onboarding-wizard`
- `fix/session-cookie-expiry`
- `chore/bump-eslint-9`
- `docs/architecture-decision-record-007`

## One concern per branch

Keep branches short-lived and single-purpose. If you find yourself making unrelated changes, stop and open a second branch. Bundling multiple concerns makes PRs harder to review and harder to revert cleanly.

### Conventional Commits


# Conventional Commits

Use [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) for every commit.

## Format

```
type(scope): short description

[optional body]

[optional footer — e.g. Closes #123]
```

## Allowed types

`feat`, `fix`, `chore`, `docs`, `style`, `refactor`, `test`, `ci`, `perf`, `revert`

## Rules

- Subject in **lower-case**, no trailing period.
- Max header length: **100 characters**.
- Body lines wrapped at **72 characters**.
- Scope is optional but recommended: a short noun for the affected module, e.g. `bin`, `claude`, `waybar`, `nvim`.

## Examples

- `feat(waybar): add startup delay drop-in`
- `fix(bin): handle missing abrt-cli gracefully`
- `chore(claude): expand settings.json allowlist`
- `docs(claude): add agentic coding conventions`

## Enforcement

Repos with `commitlint` configured will reject non-conforming commits. Use `commitlint --edit $1` in `.husky/commit-msg`.

### Pull request rules


# Pull request rules

## Size

Keep diffs **under 400 lines** of meaningful change per PR. Split larger work into a sequence of PRs that each cleanly stand alone.

The 400-line cap is about reviewability, not vanity. Diffs above ~400 lines start losing review quality fast — reviewers either skim or rubber-stamp.

## Title

PR title MUST follow the same [Conventional Commits](git-conventional-commits.md) format as commit messages. CI checks this.

## Description

- Reference the relevant issue: `Closes #N` or `Ref #N`.
- Explain the WHY, not just the WHAT — the diff shows what; the description tells future-you and reviewers why.
- **Keep the description current.** Any time a new commit is pushed to a PR branch, update the PR body to reflect the current state.

## CI gate

All CI checks must pass before merging. No exceptions for "small" changes.

## Self-review before pushing

Before opening the PR:
1. Run the full local test suite.
2. Run lint/typecheck.
3. `git diff <base>` — read your own diff. Would another engineer understand this in 6 months?


<!-- vault:end:conventions -->
